mechanism_narrative: Rising federal funds rates increase the discount rate for future corporate earnings, directly compressing equity valuations and generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns show positive mean returns when FEDFUNDS increases, or Rank IC remains below 0.02 over rolling 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid deterioration in the unemployment rate signals weakening labor demand and declining consumer spending capacity, which reduces corporate revenue expectations and drives negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in > 65% of instances where UNRATE rises > 0.5pp in 3 months.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in retail sales and housing starts indicates broad-based consumer demand recovery, which boosts corporate profit margins and generates positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: Joint positive deviations in RSAFS and HOUST fail to produce positive 60-day forward returns in > 55% of cases, or interaction term Rank IC < 0.01.

mechanism_narrative: An increase in the industrial production index reflects strengthening manufacturing output and capacity utilization, which signals near-term economic momentum and produces positive S&P 500 forward returns over 5 trading days.
failure_condition: INDPRO increases are followed by negative 5-day forward returns in > 60% of observations during early-cycle phases.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained consumer price inflation above 3 percent annually forces central banks to tighten policy expectations, raising real discount rates and triggering negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: CPIAUCSL above 3% YoY is associated with positive 20-day forward returns in > 58% of cases, or the signal loses predictive power when inflation expectations are anchored.

mechanism_narrative: Divergence between rising total nonfarm payrolls and falling unemployment indicates labor market tightening and wage growth, which supports consumer spending and generates positive S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: The PAYEMS-UNRATE divergence fails to predict positive 20-day returns in > 60% of instances, or the interaction effect is statistically insignificant in out-of-sample tests.

mechanism_narrative: Quarter-over-quarter growth in real gross domestic product reflects expanding aggregate demand and corporate revenue growth, which directly translates to positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: GDPC1 growth rates show no significant correlation with 60-day forward returns, or Rank IC remains below 0.02 across all rolling 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly decline in industrial production exceeding 1 percent signals a contraction in manufacturing activity and supply chain bottlenecks, which reduces near-term earnings forecasts and drives negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: INDPRO declines > 1% MoM are followed by positive 20-day forward returns in > 65% of cases, or the threshold effect disappears when controlling for monetary policy shifts.
